comment.body:
`https://static.airbridge.io/sdk/latest/airbridge.min.js` - already blocked by `||static.airbridge.io/sdk/`.
`static.groobee.io` - An analytics & remind-wall & offside reminding service - https://groobee.net/. But, I do not add this domain to prevent a potential breakage. 
`inapp.blux.ai` - I cloud not find this domain with a real Android device.

issue.body:
# Sorry. The website is infected by AdShield, Inc.

Unfortunately, It is complicated to resolve with a regular adblock rule because the website is being protected by service of Ad-Shield that is an anti-adblock company.

## Userscript Installation
Please block Ad-Shield advertisement by installing [tinyShield userscript](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield).
Please check its installation guideline written in English by visiting [this URL](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.md).

If you can understand Korean language, please refer https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.ko.md.

<details>
<summary>Detailed description for iOS users</summary>

1. Install [**Usercripts**](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/userscripts/id1463298887) app
2. Enable **Usercripts** extension in Safari settings
	* iOS 18+: `System settings` => `Apps` => `Safari` => `Extensions`
	* iOS 17 and older: `System settings` => `Safari` => `Extensions`
	Find **Usercripts**, enable it and allow `On other sites` permission
3. Open the [tinyShield ](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@filteringdev/tinyshield@latest/dist/tinyShield.user.js) userscript URL in browser
4. Press the extensions icon in the address bar of Safari and select Userscripts 
5. Tap to install
6. In opened popup, scroll down and press Install button
7. Done.

</details>

## Still Have a Problem?
An issue is occurred when the userscript is applied, please report to [this issue tracker](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/issues).

comment.body:
The parent element `.ads-five` has a margin around this link.

```adblock
edumail.su##.ads-five
```

I considered blocking it with the upper rule, but I decided it is better to avoid more general blocking.

Or, it might be better to see `.ads-five` as ad leftovers. since it creates an empty 2rem vertical margin.

```diff
- edumail.su##a[href="https://rmvbg.net/?utm_source=edumailfree.com&utm_medium=banner&utm_campaign=free_background_remover"]
+ edumail.su##.ads-five
```

issue.body:
# Sorry. The website is infected by AdShield, Inc.

Unfortunately, It is complicated to resolve with a regular adblock rule because the website is being protected by service of Ad-Shield that is an anti-adblock company.

## Userscript Installation
Please block Ad-Shield advertisement by installing [tinyShield userscript](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield).
Please check its installation guideline written in English by visiting [this URL](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.md).

If you can understand Korean language, please refer https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.ko.md.

<details>
<summary>Detailed description for iOS users</summary>

1. Install [**Usercripts**](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/userscripts/id1463298887) app
2. Enable **Usercripts** extension in Safari settings
	* iOS 18+: `System settings` => `Apps` => `Safari` => `Extensions`
	* iOS 17 and older: `System settings` => `Safari` => `Extensions`
	Find **Usercripts**, enable it and allow `On other sites` permission
3. Open the [tinyShield ](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@filteringdev/tinyshield@latest/dist/tinyShield.user.js) userscript URL in browser
4. Press the extensions icon in the address bar of Safari and select Userscripts 
5. Tap to install
6. In opened popup, scroll down and press Install button
7. Done.

</details>

## Still Have a Problem?
An issue is occurred when the userscript is applied, please report to [this issue tracker](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/issues).

comment.body: Probably caused by AdGuard DNS blocking `adthrive.com`, as it triggers AdShield if `ads.adthrive.com/abd/abd.js` fails to load.

labels.description: The site detects ad blocking

labels.name: A: In progress
labels.description: Work on the issue is in progress

labels.name: A: Problematic
labels.description: Issues, which cannot be resolved easily

issue.body:
# Sorry. The website is infected by AdShield, Inc.

Unfortunately, It is complicated to resolve with a regular adblock rule because the website is being protected by service of Ad-Shield that is an anti-adblock company.

## Userscript Installation
Please block Ad-Shield advertisement by installing [tinyShield userscript](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield).
Please check its installation guideline written in English by visiting [this URL](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.md).

If you can understand Korean language, please refer https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.ko.md.

<details>
<summary>Detailed description for iOS users</summary>

1. Install [**Usercripts**](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/userscripts/id1463298887) app
2. Enable **Usercripts** extension in Safari settings
	* iOS 18+: `System settings` => `Apps` => `Safari` => `Extensions`
	* iOS 17 and older: `System settings` => `Safari` => `Extensions`
	Find **Usercripts**, enable it and allow `On other sites` permission
3. Open the [tinyShield ](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@filteringdev/tinyshield@latest/dist/tinyShield.user.js) userscript URL in browser
4. Press the extensions icon in the address bar of Safari and select Userscripts 
5. Tap to install
6. In opened popup, scroll down and press Install button
7. Done.

</details>

## Still Have a Problem?
An issue is occurred when the userscript is applied, please report to [this issue tracker](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/issues).

comment.body:
Not reproduced on my end.

Please try following actions, then check the problem again:
1. Disable all content blockers in Safari settings and enable them again;
2. Restart Safari;
3. Restart AdGuard;
4. Check for filters update in AdGuard.

### Important:
<!-- **AdGuard for iOS** -->
* Do not close AdGuard window ~60 seconds, because iOS will break configuration process.
<!-- **AdGuard for Safari** -->
* The filters update process is fully completed ~10 seconds after the manual filter update check is completed.

comment.body:
I think that for now it could be a specific one, like:
```adblock
edumail.su##.ads-five
```
If there will be more similar cases where it's required, then it can be added as generic.

> There are other similar elements like `.ads-one` and `.ads-two` which are hidden by generic rules (`##.ads-one`, `##.ads-two`), so I think that `##.ads-five` should be fine.

So, can I replace it as a generic rules in `general_elemhide.txt`? like `##.ads-two`: 8b320f5736f15233fe5b0061d27e533fc6c65adb

> By the way, I also see a not blocked cookie notice (marked on the screenshot below, I guess it depends on location), so could you please add domain to the following rule in cookie notice filter?

Thanks for the additional information! I will do it.

comment.body:
Not reproduced on my end.

Please try following actions, then check the problem again:
1. Disable all content blockers in Safari settings and enable them again;
2. Restart Safari;
3. Restart AdGuard;
4. Check for filters update in AdGuard.

### Important:
<!-- **AdGuard for iOS** -->
* Do not close AdGuard window ~60 seconds, because iOS will break configuration process.
<!-- **AdGuard for Safari** -->
* The filters update process is fully completed ~10 seconds after the manual filter update check is completed.

comment.body:
Next time you can add it add the end of the rule, it's just easier to spot the difference.
In general, for rules like:
```adblock
adserver$domain=example1.com|example2.com
```
Usually we add it at the end:
```diff
- adserver$domain=example.com|example.org
+ adserver$domain=example.com|example.org|test.com
```
For element hiding rules like:
```adblock
example.com,example.org###ads
```
At the beginning of the rule:
```diff
- example.com,example.org###ads
+ test.com,example.com,example.org###ads
```

labels.name: A: Problematic

issue.body:
# Sorry. The website is infected by AdShield, Inc.

Unfortunately, It is complicated to resolve with a regular adblock rule because the website is being protected by service of Ad-Shield that is an anti-adblock company.

## Userscript Installation
Please block Ad-Shield advertisement by installing [tinyShield userscript](https://github.com/List-KR/tinyShield).
Please check its installation guideline written in English by visiting [this URL](https://github.com/List-KR/tinyShield/blob/main/README.md).

If you can understand Korean language, please refer https://github.com/List-KR/tinyShield/blob/main/README.ko.md.

<details>
<summary>Detailed description for iOS users</summary>

1. Install [**Usercripts**](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/userscripts/id1463298887) app
2. Enable **Usercripts** extension in Safari settings
	* iOS 18+: `System settings` => `Apps` => `Safari` => `Extensions`
	* iOS 17 and older: `System settings` => `Safari` => `Extensions`
	Find **Usercripts**, enable it and allow `On other sites` permission
4. Open the [tinyShield ](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@list-kr/tinyshield@latest/dist/tinyShield.user.js) userscript URL in browser
5. Press the extensions icon in the address bar of Safari and select Userscripts 
6. Tap to install
7. In opened popup, scroll down and press Install button
8. Done.

</details>

## Still Have a Problem?
An issue is occurred when the userscript is applied, please report to [this issue tracker](https://github.com/List-KR/tinyShield/issues).

comment.body:
Not reproduced on my end.

Please try following actions, then check the problem again:
1. Disable all content blockers in Safari settings and enable them again;
2. Restart Safari;
3. Restart AdGuard;
4. Check for filters update in AdGuard.

### Important:
<!-- **AdGuard for iOS** -->
* Do not close AdGuard window ~60 seconds, because iOS will break configuration process.
<!-- **AdGuard for Safari** -->
* The filters update process is fully completed ~10 seconds after the manual filter update check is completed.

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking)
 [https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/*](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Flink.mail.beehiiv.com%2Fss%2Fc%2F*)
### Comment
> Beehiiv newsletter platform uses tracking links that are blocked by Adguard DNS. This creates a friction point where users must manually disable their DNS protection just to open a link from an email. While these trackers are intrusive, they are technically required for the links to function, making them an unavoidable part of the user experience. There are many other tracking links which are not blocked by Adguard DNS default list, and I would suggest this URL should join them.
```
Username: @XChrispy
```

### Screenshots
<details>
  <summary>Screenshot 1 </summary>

  ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/lntin97mplc8oowosc8o0w8ccwows0srf2h51ma09w.png?nc=1)
</details>

  ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/o84mrzz6rmswg04wcgkkkgkcgs0cwcs2gjw8j55r6j.png?nc=1)
</details>

Information | Value
--- | ---

DNS setup: | Device or router settings

System version: | `Android 16`
Browser: | Chrome

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking)
 [https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/*](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Flink.mail.beehiiv.com%2Fss%2Fc%2F*)
### Comment
> Beehiiv newsletter platform uses tracking links that are blocked by Adguard DNS. This creates a friction point where users must manually disable their DNS protection just to open a link from an email. While these trackers are intrusive, they are technically required for the links to function, making them an unavoidable part of the user experience. There are many other tracking links which are not blocked by Adguard DNS default list, and I would suggest this URL should join them.
```
Username: @XChrispy
```

comment.body:
Добавьте в пользовательский фильтр DNS:

```
|yandex.ru|
```

Это заблокирует рекламу, т.к. они загружают её с использованием главного домена - `https://yandex.ru/ads/`

Но это вызовет проблемы на сервисах Яндекса, если вы их используете.

issue.title: ad.ari.lt false positive

labels.name: A: In progress
labels.description: Work on the issue is in progress

issue.body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] This site DOES NOT contain sexually explicit material, otherwise use [NSFW-specific form](./new?template=bug_report_NSFW.yml);
- [x] Filters were updated before reproducing an issue;
- [x] AdGuard product version is up-to-date;
- [x] Browser version is up-to-date;
- [x] If the site or app is broken, disabling AdGuard protection resolves an issue.

### What product do you use?

Other ad blocker

### AdGuard version

Filter-2-base

### What type of problem have you encountered?

Website or app doesn't work properly

### Which browser(s) do you use?

other (specify in comment below)

### Which device do you use?

Desktop

### Where is the problem encountered?

https://ad.ari.lt/

### Ad Blocking

AdGuard Base filter

### Privacy

EasyPrivacy

### Social Widgets

_No response_

### Annoyances

_No response_

### Security

_No response_

### Other

_No response_

### DNS filters

_No response_

### Language-specific

_No response_

### What Stealth Mode options do you have enabled?

_No response_

### Add your comment and screenshots

Hi,

Your block list https://raw.githubusercontent.com/AdguardTeam/FiltersRegistry/master/filters/filter_2_Base/filter.txt blocks ad.ari.lt falsely and I would like to report a false positive.

I am the author of https://ad.ari.lt/ (Arivertisements), and I would like to clarify that Arivertisements is not an advertising platform or ad network. Arivertisements is a small open source indie project inspired by https://john.citrons.xyz/ (Johnvertisements). The purpose of this "platform" is simply to allow indie websites, individuals, and alike to embed and share silly images in a lightweight, security-, privacy-, and transparency-respecting way.

Because of the nature of this project, I was surprised to hear a report from users that ad.ari.lt got blocked whereas Johnvertisements, which serves a very similar (dare I say near-exact, outside of the FOSS philosophy things) purpose and is significantly more widely used across the web, does not appear to be blocked.

Arivertisements was designed with privacy, security, transparency, supporting the indie web, and minimalism as core philosophical goals. It intentionally avoids the typical behaviours associated with advertising infrastructure, for example:

- no JavaScript in embeds (restricted iframe only)
- no tracking cookies
- no analytics or telemetry
- no fingerprinting or browser detection
- no third-party requests
- no corporate advertisements

The only optional parameter is `from=`, which allows participating websites to identify themselves for a public leaderboard of participating sites. This parameter is relatively anonymous and is not associated with IP addresses, user identifiers, psychographic profiles, or anything that a standard advertising platform does - it's just like Johnvertisements.

It is also important to note that the code for Arivertisements is open source at https://git.ari.lt/ari.lt/ad.ari.lt` (backend) and `https://git.ari.lt/ari.lt/arivertisements (images) while Johnvertisements is closed source.

Anyway, in practice, Arivertisements simply serves static images intended for fun community participation on the indie web. Its function is closer to a webring or memes than a standard advertising platform, and it does not promote products, track users, deliver targeted content, etc. - the only bias is for newer images to make the rotation more interesting and encourage contribution. It is also explicitly anti-corporate considering the CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 and AGPL-3.0 licensing.

I would appreciate a review to determine whether the block is appropriate and correct given the project's minimal, independent, and privacy-respecting design.

### Privacy

- [x] I agree to follow this condition

issue.title: ad.ari.lt false positive

labels.name: A: In progress
labels.description: Work on the issue is in progress

issue.body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] This site DOES NOT contain sexually explicit material, otherwise use [NSFW-specific form](./new?template=bug_report_NSFW.yml);
- [x] Filters were updated before reproducing an issue;
- [x] AdGuard product version is up-to-date;
- [x] Browser version is up-to-date;
- [x] If the site or app is broken, disabling AdGuard protection resolves an issue.

### What product do you use?

Other ad blocker

### AdGuard version

Filter-2-base

### What type of problem have you encountered?

Website or app doesn't work properly

### Which browser(s) do you use?

other (specify in comment below)

### Which device do you use?

Desktop

### Where is the problem encountered?

https://ad.ari.lt/

### Ad Blocking

AdGuard Base filter

### Privacy

EasyPrivacy

### Social Widgets

_No response_

### Annoyances

_No response_

### Security

_No response_

### Other

_No response_

### DNS filters

_No response_

### Language-specific

_No response_

### What Stealth Mode options do you have enabled?

_No response_

### Add your comment and screenshots

Hi,

Your block list https://raw.githubusercontent.com/AdguardTeam/FiltersRegistry/master/filters/filter_2_Base/filter.txt blocks ad.ari.lt falsely and I would like to report a false positive.

I am the author of https://ad.ari.lt/ (Arivertisements), and I would like to clarify that Arivertisements is not an advertising platform or ad network. Arivertisements is a small open source indie project inspired by https://john.citrons.xyz/ (Johnvertisements). The purpose of this "platform" is simply to allow indie websites, individuals, and alike to embed and share silly images in a lightweight, security-, privacy-, and transparency-respecting way.

Because of the nature of this project, I was surprised to hear a report from users that ad.ari.lt got blocked whereas Johnvertisements, which serves a very similar (dare I say near-exact, outside of the FOSS philosophy things) purpose and is significantly more widely used across the web, does not appear to be blocked.

Arivertisements was designed with privacy, security, transparency, supporting the indie web, and minimalism as core philosophical goals. It intentionally avoids the typical behaviours associated with advertising infrastructure, for example:

- no JavaScript in embeds (restricted iframe only)
- no tracking cookies
- no analytics or telemetry
- no fingerprinting or browser detection
- no third-party requests
- no corporate advertisements

The only optional parameter is `from=`, which allows participating websites to identify themselves for a public leaderboard of participating sites. This parameter is relatively anonymous and is not associated with IP addresses, user identifiers, psychographic profiles, or anything that a standard advertising platform does - it's just like Johnvertisements.

It is also important to note that the code for Arivertisements is open source at https://git.ari.lt/ari.lt/ad.ari.lt` (backend) and `https://git.ari.lt/ari.lt/arivertisements (images) while Johnvertisements is closed source.

Anyway, in practice, Arivertisements simply serves static images intended for fun community participation on the indie web. Its function is closer to a webring or memes than a standard advertising platform, and it does not promote products, track users, deliver targeted content, etc. - the only bias is for newer images to make the rotation more interesting and encourage contribution. It is also explicitly anti-corporate considering the CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 and AGPL-3.0 licensing.

I would appreciate a review to determine whether the block is appropriate and correct given the project's minimal, independent, and privacy-respecting design.

### Privacy

- [x] I agree to follow this condition

comment.body: Hi! In fact, it's blocked by the `EasyList` which is part of our AdGuard Base. I have contacted to author, so let's wait for the answer. I've also unblocked it for now, so update should be available soon.

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Ads) [https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.dubox.drive](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fplay.google.com%2Fstore%2Fapps%2Fdetails%3Fid%3Dcom.dubox.drive) ### Comment > Desde hace algunos días estoy usando la app TeraBox (https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.dubox.drive) y me estoy dando cuenta que a pesar de que AdGuard está en funcionamiento se muestran esporádicamente anuncios, se queda activo el espacio de la publicidad que la versión estándar muestra, cosa que en otras aplicaciones con publicidad ese espacio queda vacío y sólo aparece un espacio vacío en negro, ya revisé la configuración de AdGuard pero no encuentro alguna forma de evitar ésto. ### Issue URL (Ads) [https://magmix.jp/photo/340340?utm_source=news.yahoo.co.jp&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=340340_6#photo_extra1](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fmagmix.jp%2Fphoto%2F340340%3Futm_source%3Dnews.yahoo.co.jp%26utm_medium%3Dreferral%26utm_campaign%3D340340_6%23photo_extra1) ### Comment > すり抜け広告の報告です。 > > このサイトの記事画像ページを開くと、上部に広告ふたつ(これはスクロールでスルーできる)、下部には動かせない&消せないバナー広告がでます。 > > サイトトップでは広告は出ませんが、記事画像ページだけでなく記事本体のほうにも同様に広告が出ています。 > 改善されると嬉しいです。 ### Screenshots
